name: First Western Bank & Trust
description_short: Community bank serving 19 locations across North Dakota, South Dakota, Minnesota, and Arizona since 1964, offering personal, business, agricultural, and mortgage banking services with FDIC insurance.
description_long:
First Western Bank & Trust has operated as a community-focused financial institution for over 60 years, establishing itself as a trusted banking partner since its founding in 1964. The bank maintains 19 physical locations across four states (North Dakota, South Dakota, Minnesota, and Arizona), positioning itself as a regional community bank rather than a national institution. The bank emphasizes deep local knowledge combined with modern banking technology to serve its customer base.

The company offers a comprehensive suite of banking services including personal checking and savings accounts, money market accounts, business banking solutions with cash management tools, agricultural lending with specialized expertise, mortgage loans for home purchase and refinance, and trust and estate planning services. They provide digital banking through online platforms and a mobile app (MyFirstWestern), credit card products (Cashback Visa with no annual fee), and the ability to open accounts remotely through their OpenAnywhere™ service. Additional services include tax eStatements, fraud detection, and purchase security protection on credit cards.

First Western distinguishes itself through its agricultural banking focus, acknowledging the unique needs of farming operations with specialized lending products and financial guidance. The bank emphasizes community reinvestment through initiatives like their annual "100 Cheers" grocery program and positions itself around personalized service rather than transactional banking. They highlight FDIC insurance backing and modern technology integration while maintaining the personal touch of a community bank.

As a regional community bank, First Western serves customers seeking local relationship banking with modern digital capabilities. However, their geographic footprint is limited to the Upper Midwest and Arizona, and their service offerings, while comprehensive, may lack some specialized products available at larger national banks. Customers should verify service availability at their specific location among the 19 branches. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Personal checking and savings accounts with flexible terms
Business banking and cash management services
Mortgage loans for home purchase, refinance, and customized options
Cashback Visa credit card with rewards and fraud protection
MyCardStatement online credit card payment service
Certificate of Deposit (CDs) implied through money market products
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
19 physical branch locations across 4 states for in-person banking services
Specialized agricultural lending with dedicated ag banking experts
Cashback Visa credit card with no annual fee and fraud detection service
OpenAnywhere™ service allows account opening online within minutes, 24/7
Comprehensive trust and estate planning services for legacy planning
Strong community reinvestment programs including annual charitable initiatives
Mobile app (MyFirstWestern) and online banking for digital account management
cons: Limited geographic footprint to Upper Midwest and Arizona—not available nationally
Smaller institution may have fewer specialized products than major national banks
Only 19 locations could limit convenience for customers outside service areas
Website does not clearly display current interest rates for deposit or loan products
No mention of specific loan approval timelines or processing procedures
best_for: Agricultural business owners and farmers in the Upper Midwest seeking specialized ag lending
Families and individuals in North Dakota, South Dakota, Minnesota, or Arizona wanting community-focused relationship banking
Small business owners requiring personalized cash management and business lending services
Customers prioritizing FDIC-insured deposits and local decision-making over large national bank options
diagnosis: First Western Bank & Trust is best suited for customers in the Upper Midwest and Arizona seeking traditional community banking with FDIC insurance, local relationship management, and specialized agricultural lending expertise. The main caveat is geographic limitation—they only serve 19 locations across four states, making them unavailable for national coverage, and prospective customers should verify branch availability in their area.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: Community bank with 19 locations in North Dakota, South Dakota, Minnesota, and Arizona since 1964, offering personal, business, and agricultural banking.
